build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player signs artists from a scouting pool by paying an advance. Each artist has a genre, talent rating, and morale. Signed artists appear on the label roster. Score 0 if artists cannot be signed, if there is no scouting pool, or if signing is free. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Albums are produced by allocating studio time to an artist. Production quality depends on time invested and producer style chosen. Completed albums generate revenue from sales over time. Score 0 if albums cannot be produced, if production has no quality variation, or if albums generate no revenue. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Marketing budget is allocated across channels (social media, radio, press, touring) for each release. Marketing spend affects album sales and artist fanbase growth. Score 0 if marketing does not exist or if it has no effect on sales. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A money economy drives the label: revenue comes from album sales and tours, expenses include studio rent, advances, marketing, and salaries. A money counter is permanently visible. Score 0 if there is no money system or if the label cannot go bankrupt. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> new label -> sign/produce/market/tour -> weeks advance -> quarterly results. Time advances week by week with genre trends shifting. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no time progression. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least four music genres exist (rock, pop, electronic, hip-hop) with different trend cycles. Genre trends are visible on a chart and affect album sales timing. Score 0 if genres do not exist or if trends have no effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Artists have distinct personalities, talent levels, and morale states. At least four artists are available to scout with different genre specialties. Score 0 if all artists are identical or if fewer than three are available. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Artist morale is affected by workload, tour scheduling, and album success. Low morale reduces album quality and may cause artists to leave. Score 0 if morale does not exist or if it has no gameplay effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Tours can be scheduled for artists, generating revenue and growing fanbases but costing money and draining morale. Tour success depends on fanbase size and marketing. Score 0 if tours do not exist or if they have no trade-off. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Multiple marketing channels have different effectiveness for different genres and diminishing returns. The player must choose where to allocate limited budget strategically. Score 0 if only one marketing option exists or if all channels are identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The HUD shows money, label reputation, genre trend chart, and current week in clear non-overlapping regions. Artist roster is accessible. Score 0 if money, trends, or time is missing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The dashboard clearly shows signed artists with portraits, album status, marketing allocation, and financial summary. Different management screens are navigable and distinct. Score 0 if artist info is unreadable or if screens are indistinguishable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Actions produce visible feedback: signing shows artist join roster, production shows progress bar, releases show sales charts, marketing shows reach indicators, and morale changes show artist reactions. Score 0 if actions produce no observable change. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: dashboard, artist cards, charts, album covers, and UI belong to a consistent music-industry visual language with modern creative-studio tones. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Artist portraits are real illustrated or pixel-art characters with distinct appearances per artist. Album covers are illustrated objects, not plain text. Score 0 if artists have no portraits or if albums are unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The dashboard and management screens use real illustrated backgrounds suggesting a recording studio or office environment. Charts and graphs are styled with themed colors. Score 0 if backgrounds are flat colored fills. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with music-industry chrome: artist cards have record-sleeve styling, financial displays have ledger aesthetics, and menus have studio-panel backgrounds. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and atmosphere: trend charts animate, sales numbers tick up, artist morale changes show reactions, album releases have launch effects, and the dashboard has ambient activity (equalizer bars, notification pulses). Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
